steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify the right triangle structure and legs
steps.content: Triangle XYZ has a right angle at Y, so XY and YZ are the two legs. We know YZ = 26 units. The area of a right triangle is $\frac{1}{2} \times \text{leg}_1 \times \text{leg}_2$, so we need to find XY to calculate the area.

steps.title: Express the unknown leg using tangent at angle Z
steps.content:
At angle Z (33°), the opposite side is XY and the adjacent side is YZ = 26. By the tangent ratio:
$$\tan(33^{\circ}) = \frac{XY}{26}$$
Solving for XY:
$$XY = 26\tan(33^{\circ})$$

steps.title: Calculate area and solve for k
steps.content:
Now substitute both legs into the area formula:
$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times YZ \times XY = \frac{1}{2} \times 26 \times 26\tan(33^{\circ})$$
$$= \frac{1}{2} \times 676 \times \tan(33^{\circ}) = 338\tan(33^{\circ})$$
Comparing to the form $k\tan(33^{\circ})$, we have $k = 338$.
